frameworks.name: spatial working memory (delayed match / memory-guided saccade)
frameworks.one_line: retention-interval activity bridges target encoding and overt response

frameworks.name: primate single-unit comparative framework
frameworks.one_line: human MEG mapped against monkey parietal-prefrontal delay-cell findings

frameworks.name: oscillatory-activity modulation
frameworks.one_line: stimulus-driven and sustained oscillations index attention and memory

core_question: Which human cortical signals during a spatial working memory delay reflect attention, memory, and motor preparation rather than the movement itself?
key_assumptions: MEG source estimates approximate underlying cortical currents
delay-period activity in primates indexes attention/memory independent of movement type
same-different judgement vs memory-guided saccade dissociates movement-related from mnemonic activity
manipulations: response modality: finger-lift same-different judgement vs memory-guided saccade
retention interval (~3 s) between target and probe
target spatial position to be remembered
key_findings: strong stimulus-driven transient and sustained MEG responses observed
modulations of oscillatory activity during the retention period
current sources spread widely over Rolandic, parietal, occipital, and frontal cortex
interpretations: some delay-period activity reflects attention/memory processes, others reflect motor preparation
human MEG signatures are consistent with primate single-unit delay-activity findings
limitations_noted: current source estimates from MEG are coarse
connecting_signals: attention vs memory vs motor preparation
